response:
source
The function begins by initializing two variables to hold the sum of squares and the product of cubes, setting the latter to one to satisfy the empty list requirement.
***
It then iterates through each integer in the provided slice, calculating the square of the current number and adding it to the running total for the sum.
***
Simultaneously within the same loop, the function computes the cube of the current number and multiplies it with the existing product value.
***
If the input slice contains no elements, the loop never executes, leaving the sum at zero and the product at one as specified by the problem constraints.
***
After processing all items, the function returns a two-element integer array where the first index holds the sum of squares and the second holds the product of cubes.
***
This implementation ensures that the mathematical operations are performed correctly for any set of whole numbers while adhering to the edge case rules defined in the prompt.
